title: Diosma berzeliifolia

intro: Diosma berzeliifolia, commonly known as the Cape plumbago or wild marigold, is a shrubby, evergreen plant native to South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include the potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Diosma berzeliifolia include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Diosma is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and capsules, although its use as a medicinal plant is not as widespread as some other species, and more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
